jokes.text: I only took this nanny job so he'd give me a free divorce, and then marry me, so I'd never have to work again.
jokes.setup: Lindsay explaining her career motivation
jokes.punchline: The circular logic of taking a job to never work again
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - only Lindsay would have this twisted logic about work. Strong structure with the contradiction.

jokes.text: I've always wanted to make love on a horse.
jokes.setup: Hope innocently talking about her horse riding progress
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate sexual comment from a child
jokes.explanation: Shocking and uncomfortable in the best Arrested Development way. Economy is perfect - maximum awkwardness in minimal words.

jokes.text: That's so funny, because I can put my leg behind my head.
jokes.setup: Lindsay trying to compete with Hope's riding skills
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's inappropriately sexual response to a child
jokes.explanation: Classic Lindsay - competitive but completely missing the inappropriateness. Great character work.

jokes.text: Of course. Who wouldn't want to snog the nanny?
jokes.setup: Lindsay's self-awareness about nanny fantasies
jokes.punchline: Her complete lack of self-awareness about why she's failing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lindsay's misplaced confidence, but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Michael had recently fallen bum-over-noggin for a beautiful Englishwoman called Rita.
jokes.setup: Narrator explaining Michael's infatuation
jokes.punchline: Using British idiom 'bum-over-noggin' instead of 'head-over-heels'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that sets up the British theme. Rewards attention and fits the show's linguistic humor.

jokes.text: Did I say 'snog' again? Oh, bloody hell.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to impress Rita with British slang
jokes.punchline: His self-awareness about trying too hard
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Michael's awkward attempts to connect with Rita.

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Did I miss a call?
jokes.setup: Michael telling Lindsay not to act desperate
jokes.punchline: Lindsay immediately proving his point by frantically checking her phone
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff with immediate contradiction. Classic Lindsay behavior.

jokes.text: God, is that how I look?
jokes.setup: Michael racing desperately to the station
jokes.punchline: Michael seeing his own desperation reflected in someone else
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy setup with self-awareness. Visual gag works well.

jokes.text: You're right. Do not answer that.
jokes.setup: Michael asking Rita invasive questions about her trip
jokes.punchline: Immediately realizing and correcting his clingy behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's self-awareness, but fairly standard.

jokes.text: I wasn't hurrying to come stop you. I was out on for my daily, uh...
jokes.setup: Michael trying to cover for his obvious desperation
jokes.punchline: His transparent lie falling apart mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe humor as Michael digs himself deeper.

jokes.text: I was in the Olympics once. I won a silver medal.
jokes.setup: Rita casually mentioning Olympic participation
jokes.punchline: The revelation that she's famous athlete Rita Leeds
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and setup for later developments. Economical revelation.

jokes.text: And the silver medal. Yes, and you came in second.
jokes.setup: Rita mentioning her silver medal
jokes.punchline: Michael stating the obvious about what silver medal means
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from Michael's obvious statement. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: But you take my breath away.
jokes.setup: Michael being embarrassed about being out of breath in front of an athlete
jokes.punchline: Turning his embarrassment into a romantic line
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay that shows Michael's quick thinking. Good recovery moment.

jokes.text: You're such (bleep).
jokes.setup: Rita appearing to insult Michael with a bleeped word
jokes.punchline: The setup for the narrator's explanation about cultural differences
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that sets up one of the episode's running gags. Perfect structure and economy.

jokes.text: Michael was stunned. He assumed Rita was using the word (bleep) in the American sense, meaning weak or cowardly. In fact, Rita meant it in the British sense, meaning sweet or gentle, as in 'pussycat.'
jokes.setup: The apparent insult from Rita
jokes.punchline: Revealing the cultural misunderstanding about 'pussy'
jokes.explanation: Masterful wordplay that becomes the episode's central conceit. Brilliant structure and will pay off throughout.

jokes.text: Fighting for your country-- you're such a pussy.
jokes.setup: WWII movie scene illustrating British usage
jokes.punchline: Soldier being called 'pussy' as a compliment in British context
jokes.explanation: Great visual callback that reinforces the cultural difference joke with historical context.

jokes.text: When I miss your lips, I'll put a (bleep) in my mouth and think of you.
jokes.setup: Continuing the WWII movie example
jokes.punchline: Another innocent British usage that sounds sexual to Americans
jokes.explanation: Escalates the running gag perfectly. Maximum discomfort with perfect economy.

jokes.text: That also had a different meaning over there.
jokes.setup: The extremely awkward movie line
jokes.punchline: Narrator's understated acknowledgment
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan capper to the uncomfortable moment. Classic narrator understatement.

jokes.text: But we're not good at that stuff.
jokes.setup: Michael suggesting they do athletic activities
jokes.punchline: George Michael's matter-of-fact acknowledgment of their inadequacy
jokes.explanation: Perfect George Michael honesty. Great character integration and deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: In fact, the only thing George Michael really liked was hanging motionless from the monkey bars. Which the President's Council on Fitness ranks as 'slightly easier than the slide.'
jokes.setup: George Michael's athletic limitations
jokes.punchline: The absurd official ranking of hanging as barely more difficult than sliding
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor with perfect specificity. The fake government ranking is inspired.

jokes.text: Yeah, who's Ann?
jokes.setup: George Michael mentioning his girlfriend Ann
jokes.punchline: Michael's ongoing inability to remember or recognize Ann
jokes.explanation: Classic running gag execution. Perfect economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: No, I know her name's not Annabelle. That's how I remember her name, 'cause her body's kind of shaped like a...
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his memory technique for Ann's name
jokes.punchline: The implication that her body is bell-shaped
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline structure with body humor. Works for Michael's character.

jokes.text: She's the belle of the ball.
jokes.setup: Michael catching himself making a body comment
jokes.punchline: Recovering with the 'belle of the ball' pun
jokes.explanation: Nice recovery and wordplay. Shows Michael's quick thinking to avoid offense.

jokes.text: She's... she running the lights or something?
jokes.setup: Learning Ann is in a beauty pageant
jokes.punchline: Michael's assumption she must be working behind the scenes
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor showing Michael's tactlessness. Character-appropriate awkwardness.

jokes.text: Well, it's half. It's at the State Fair, which this year is gonna be a Church and State Fair.
jokes.setup: Discussion of the beauty pageant being Christian-themed
jokes.punchline: The literal 'Church and State' fair name
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on church/state separation. Sets up later plot developments.

jokes.text: There's no such thing as an inner beauty pageant. They're just gonna pick the chick with the best T and A.
jokes.setup: Discussion of the 'inner beauty' pageant
jokes.punchline: Maeby's cynical but accurate assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Maeby cynicism. Good observational humor that sets up her arc.

jokes.text: As a child, Maeby had been entered into a number of progressive pageants that claimed to celebrate beauty in all its forms. But the winners still tended to have it 'going on' in the conventional sense.
jokes.setup: Explaining Maeby's pageant history
jokes.punchline: The ironic reality that 'progressive' pageants still picked conventionally attractive winners
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary delivered with perfect narrator tone. Good setup for Maeby's motivations.

jokes.text: I don't know where that hair of yours came from. Shameless Plugs, on Placentia. Looks pretty good, huh? And they're not licensed, which means big savings.
jokes.setup: Lindsay commenting on Maeby's hair
jokes.punchline: Turning it into a detailed advertisement for an unlicensed hair salon
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment. Perfect Lindsay behavior - turning a compliment into inappropriate product placement.

jokes.text: I'm guessing 'have sex', but that's meeting you more than halfway.
jokes.setup: Lindsay's euphemism about Bob wanting to 'crown' her
jokes.punchline: Maeby's deadpan translation and complaint about effort
jokes.explanation: Perfect Maeby deadpan. Excellent economy and the 'meeting you halfway' complaint is inspired.

jokes.text: I'll show them a little T and A. Tobias and Ann.
jokes.setup: Tobias offering to help with the pageant
jokes.punchline: Revealing that 'T and A' means 'Tobias and Ann'
jokes.explanation: Masterful wordplay that's perfectly Tobias - oblivious to sexual meanings but creating accidental double entendres.

jokes.text: Yeah, I think you just did.
jokes.setup: Tobias saying he'll 'let out' the queen in Ann
jokes.punchline: George Michael's uncomfortable reaction to the sexual implication
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing George Michael's discomfort with sexual innuendo about his girlfriend.

jokes.text: I heard the jury's still out on science.
jokes.setup: Discussion of DNA test proving Steve Holt is Gob's son
jokes.punchline: Gob's dismissal of scientific evidence
jokes.explanation: Iconic Gob line that perfectly captures his delusional thinking. Became a widely quoted phrase.

jokes.text: Come on. I lie to yours all the time.
jokes.setup: Michael refusing to lie to Steve Holt
jokes.punchline: Gob's casual admission of lying to George Michael
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Gob's casual dishonesty and lack of boundaries.

jokes.text: Second place is someone weird usually, like a Chinese girl or a geologist.
jokes.setup: Gob explaining pageant dynamics
jokes.punchline: His bizarre categorization of 'weird' contestants
jokes.explanation: Typical Gob casual prejudice disguised as pageant wisdom. Character-appropriate but problematic.

jokes.text: So I step in and, uh, lay her crown upon my sweet head.
jokes.setup: Gob explaining his strategy with third-place contestants
jokes.punchline: The sexual euphemism about 'laying her crown'
jokes.explanation: Typical Gob sexual bragging with creative euphemism. Character-appropriate crude humor.

jokes.text: Have I missed this euphemism?
jokes.setup: Gob's 'crown' euphemism
jokes.punchline: Michael's confusion about whether it's actually a euphemism
jokes.explanation: Nice meta moment where Michael questions the sexual meaning. Good reaction beat.

jokes.text: You know, uh, you got to stop saying your name all the time.
jokes.setup: Steve Holt's habit of shouting his own name
jokes.punchline: Michael's direct address of the bizarre behavior
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about Steve Holt's quirk. Michael calling it out directly.

jokes.text: Only for a wimp, a wuss, a (bleep). And Steve didn't mean pussycat.
jokes.setup: Steve calling the triathlon tough only for certain types
jokes.punchline: Narrator clarifying Steve means the American version of the word
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to the episode's central joke. Perfect narrator clarification.

jokes.text: Uncle Mike! Steve Holt! Both men felt good about themselves. Uncle Mike! Steve Holt! But Gob didn't share their enthusiasm.
jokes.setup: Michael and Steve celebrating their partnership
jokes.punchline: The absurd visual of them chanting each other's names while Gob watches
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy with the contrasting emotions. Nice character work showing Gob's jealousy.

jokes.text: So this is how Michael gets rid of my son? By stealing him? Well, I'll just have to steal something of his. Like, say, his bicycle. As a... placeholder.
jokes.setup: Gob feeling betrayed by Michael taking Steve Holt
jokes.punchline: His petty revenge plan and weak justification
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Gob's childish logic and petty revenge impulses.

jokes.text: Oh, I can't believe I chose this moment to call you.
jokes.setup: Michael training hard and being out of breath
jokes.punchline: Calling Rita while completely winded from exercise
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy of bad timing. Good cringe moment but not exceptional.

jokes.text: It's Surely.
jokes.setup: Someone calling her Maeby
jokes.punchline: Corrects to fake name 'Surely'
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Maeby's disguise plot. 'Surely' is a funny fake name choice.

jokes.text: Just watch, Surely's inner beauty will get trumped by her outer icky.
jokes.setup: Maeby explaining her pageant strategy as 'Surely'
jokes.punchline: The blunt 'outer icky' description contrasted with pageant language
jokes.explanation: Good Maeby cynicism with nice wordplay. 'Outer icky' is a funny coinage.

jokes.text: It had been purchased for her at a charity auction she'd attended as a studio executive, $10,000 by Jamie Kennedy.
jokes.setup: Explaining where Maeby got the Nicole Kidman nose
jokes.punchline: The specific detail about Jamie Kennedy buying it for $10,000
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist detail about the nose's provenance. Jamie Kennedy reference adds specificity.

jokes.text: But I'm not putting you in my remake of A Thoroughly Polite Dustup.
jokes.setup: Jamie Kennedy at the charity auction
jokes.punchline: The absurd British-sounding movie title
jokes.explanation: Funny fake movie title that sounds appropriately British and polite. Good absurdist touch.

jokes.text: My God, he's already lost a hand. If he loses a leg, how will we ever get him out of the apartment?
jokes.setup: Discussing Buster's military service
jokes.punchline: Lucille's selfish concern about mobility rather than Buster's welfare
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille selfishness. Dark humor about disability with excellent character integration.

jokes.text: Where's your hand? Oh, it got stuck in the prize hole again. I told you not to play that stupid game anymore. Ah! There goes number three.
jokes.setup: Buster's missing hand
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's lost multiple hands to the same prize machine
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Buster's hand problems. Perfect absurdist logic and character consistency.

jokes.text: You'll do anything to get out of (bleep) in this apartment. Ah, you're drunk.
jokes.setup: George Sr. saying he'll do anything to get out
jokes.punchline: The misheard/mispronounced version creating different meaning
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the episode's central wordplay theme. Nice misdirection and structure.

jokes.text: 'At Risk Male Youth.' That spells 'Army.'
jokes.setup: Discussion of the 'Startled Straight' program
jokes.punchline: Buster noticing the acronym spells ARMY
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay discovery that reveals the military recruitment angle. Perfect economy and structure.

jokes.text: And that's when Buster discovered a recruiting pool the government had been fishing for years.
jokes.setup: Buster's revelation about the ARMY acronym
jokes.punchline: The implication of military targeting at-risk youth with the 'fishing' metaphor
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary disguised as exposition. Good wordplay with 'fishing' metaphor.

jokes.text: Actually, Lucille went down faster.
jokes.setup: Comparing triathlon bodily function loss to Lucille on New Year's Eve
jokes.punchline: Narrator's deadpan comparison
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about Lucille's drinking. Classic narrator understatement.

jokes.text: Have sex with this girl right now. Do it, go. Get in there. Have some sex with her right now. I didn't think so.
jokes.setup: Tobias claiming he's not afraid of sex
jokes.punchline: Maeby calling his bluff with impossible demands
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor with Maeby's direct challenge to Tobias's sexual confidence.

jokes.text: You can control your bladder when you're dead. No blood, no oil! There's no 'I' in 'win.'
jokes.setup: Exercise motivation speech
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd and incorrectly remembered motivational sayings
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist motivational speech parody. 'No I in win' is particularly funny malapropism.

jokes.text: I thought he was older, and... more manly.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why he chose Steve Holt over George Michael
jokes.punchline: Accidentally insulting his son's manliness
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Michael's tactlessness. Realistic family awkwardness.

jokes.text: Hey, does anyone know why my handlebars are in my bed?!
jokes.setup: Gob's bicycle theft revenge
jokes.punchline: Michael discovering the disassembled bike parts
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy callback. Nice escalation of Gob's petty revenge.

jokes.text: They're, uh, pills filled with oxygen. They keep you from being incontinent. Have one, they're called Oxy-Incontinent.
jokes.setup: Steve offering Michael performance enhancement pills
jokes.punchline: The malapropism creating 'Oxy-Incontinent' instead of OxyContin
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that works on multiple levels. Perfect character integration and sets up later complications.

jokes.text: About just as much as Big Bird and the Keebler elves.
jokes.setup: Pageant question about God's influence
jokes.punchline: Maeby's completely inappropriate and honest answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Maeby honesty in inappropriate setting. Great specific cultural references.

jokes.text: You want to have some guy reach around you in the middle of the night, start messing with your junk?
jokes.setup: George Sr. in the wrong 'Startled Straight' tent
jokes.punchline: His unintentionally sexual description meant to discourage homosexuality
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection humor with George Sr.'s unintentional come-on. Problematic but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Is he ugly?
jokes.setup: George Sr.'s description of prison life
jokes.punchline: The audience member's completely wrong focus
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist reaction that misses the point entirely. Nice economy.

jokes.text: She can stand! The Lord's blessed her!
jokes.setup: Maeby standing up to withdraw from the pageant
jokes.punchline: The crowd thinking it's a miracle
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment with religious overtones. Good visual gag and misdirection.

jokes.text: I just thought you wanted to hang out and do guy stuff.
jokes.setup: Michael questioning why Steve let him train so hard
jokes.punchline: Steve's innocent desire for bonding
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that contrasts with Michael's competitiveness. Good emotional honesty.

jokes.text: Outgrown you. That is the term I've been looking for.
jokes.setup: George Michael worried about Ann outgrowing him
jokes.punchline: Ann seizing on the exact term to break up with him
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/payoff with George Michael providing his own breakup ammunition. Good dramatic irony.

jokes.text: Outgrown you. That is the term I've been looking for.
jokes.setup: George Michael worried Ann outgrew him
jokes.punchline: Ann confirms this is exactly what happened
jokes.explanation: Brutal efficiency. Ann's relief at finding the right words to dump him is perfect.

jokes.text: Of course, they were way older.
jokes.setup: Michael and Steve winning the father-son triathlon
jokes.punchline: Narrator pointing out they competed against actual children
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Michael's victory. Classic narrator understatement revealing the truth.

jokes.text: Hey, do this with me!
jokes.setup: Michael rushing to stop George Michael's enlistment
jokes.punchline: Still chained to Steve, dragging him along
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy callback. The chains create an urgent slapstick moment.

jokes.text: Excuse me. I can't go back, I can't go back!
jokes.setup: George Sr.'s prison description backfiring
jokes.punchline: A potential recruit being scared away from the Army toward the thing George Sr. described
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of George Sr.'s mistaken success. Nice ironic reversal.

jokes.text: And he decided to escape in the bulletproof one.
jokes.setup: George Sr. choosing between Army Humvee and Pope Mobile
jokes.punchline: Chooses Pope Mobile thinking it's bulletproof
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the tag. George Sr.'s faulty logic creates anticipation.

jokes.text: No, no, no, no, no. Come on! I can't go back.
jokes.setup: George Sr. trying to steal the Pope Mobile
jokes.punchline: His desperation leading to another escape attempt
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy with George Sr.'s panic. Sets up the next episode teaser.

jokes.text: because I could not take you being away in the Army.
jokes.setup: Michael calling himself the wimp instead of George Michael
jokes.punchline: His vulnerable admission of dependence on his son
jokes.explanation: Strong emotional moment that works as comedy through vulnerability. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: I think I'm gonna go get one of those corn dog crosses. With all the 'crucifixins'?
jokes.setup: The Church and State Fair setting
jokes.punchline: The religious pun on corn dog toppings
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay combining religious imagery with fair food. Perfect pun construction.

jokes.text: You're a complete and utter... (bleep) I can't believe we had to bleep that. It would have been such a nice moment.
jokes.setup: Rita calling Michael sweet again
jokes.punchline: Narrator's commentary on having to censor the compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that caps the episode's central joke. Meta-commentary adds layer. Brilliant structure.

jokes.text: Thank God I wasn't in the Humvee.
jokes.setup: George Sr. choosing the Pope Mobile over the Humvee
jokes.punchline: His relief at making the wrong choice for protection
jokes.explanation: Good ironic punchline for the next episode teaser. Classic George Sr. misplaced priorities.
